dashboard_grid.name: The Command Center (Bento Mode B)
dashboard_grid.description: High density grid for data correlation. No wasted pixels.
dashboard_grid.classes: grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-4 p-4 md:p-6

items.summary_card: col-span-1 bg-card border border-border p-6 rounded-lg
items.main_chart: col-span-1 md:col-span-2 lg:col-span-3 row-span-2 bg-card border border-border p-6 rounded-lg
items.feed_list: col-span-1 row-span-2 bg-card border border-border p-6 rounded-lg h-full

public_order_page.name: The Digital Receipt
public_order_page.description: Mobile-first, vertical stack, focus on trust and clarity.
public_order_page.container_classes: max-w-md mx-auto min-h-screen bg-background p-4 pb-32

buttons.primary: bg-primary text-primary-foreground hover:bg-primary/90 h-10 px-4 py-2 rounded-md font-medium transition-all active:scale-95
buttons.secondary: bg-secondary text-secondary-foreground hover:bg-secondary/80 h-10 px-4 py-2 rounded-md font-medium
buttons.ghost: hover:bg-accent hover:text-accent-foreground h-10 px-4 py-2 rounded-md font-medium
buttons.destructive: bg-destructive text-destructive-foreground hover:bg-destructive/90 h-10 px-4 py-2 rounded-md font-medium

cards.base: rounded-lg border border-border bg-card text-card-foreground shadow-sm

inputs.base: flex h-10 w-full rounded-md border border-input bg-background px-3 py-2 text-sm ring-offset-background file:border-0 file:bg-transparent file:text-sm file:font-medium placeholder:text-muted-foreground focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:ring-offset-2 disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50

products.alt: Minimalist cosmetic bottle on dark background

general: Implement the 'Dark Mode First' strategy. Ensure all default views are dark.
Use the 'Outfit' font for all headings to give a modern, brand-distinct look.
Use 'Plus Jakarta Sans' for body text to ensure high readability on mobile.
The Primary Color is #FF5500 (Orange). Use it for CTAs and active states. Do NOT use it for backgrounds unless it's a small badge or button.
For the Dashboard, strictly follow the 'Bento Grid' layout. Do not create a generic list view.
For the Public Order Page, focus on the 'Digital Receipt' aesthetic. It should feel like a native mobile app view.
Ensure all inputs and interactive elements have a minimum height of 44px for touch targets.

UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur)
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: transition: all. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add .App { text-align: center; } in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮🎯📚🎭🎬🎪🎉🎊🎁🎀🎂🍰🎈🎨🎰💰💵💳🏦💎🪙💸🤑📊📈📉💹🔢🏆🥇 etc for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories (especially sponsor logos) = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable
Create new components that match the style and conventions of existing components when needed
Examine existing components to understand the project's component patterns before creating new ones
Components MUST use named exports (export const ComponentName = ...)
Pages MUST use default exports (export default function PageName() {...})
Use sonner for toasts. Sonner component are located in /app/src/components/ui/sonner.tsx
